# GUIDELINES

1. See common [RUST.md](./DNA/languages/RUST.md) guideline
2. When develop new REST API use [API.md](./DNA/REST/API.md), [STATUS_CODES](./DNA/REST/STATUS_CODES.md)
3. When develop new Module use [ModKit Unified System](../docs/modkit_unified_system/README.md)
4. Check [SECURITY.md](./SECURITY.md) for security best practices.
5. Review [DEPENDENCIES.md](./DEPENDENCIES.md) for preferred dependencies and libraries.

item.text: The applied code adds a separate membership validator and routes only the create/update `allowed_membership_types` loops through it. The regression tests cover create/update acceptance for registered external codes plus empty and over-length rejection; I’m checking the committed context and running the exact focused suite now.

item.text: The full `type_service_test` suite is also green: 49 passed, including the new external-membership create/update coverage and the retained RG-prefixed membership path tests. No blockers surfaced in the implementation review.

arguments.summary_title: Resource-group allowed-membership type validation regression coverage passed
arguments.summary_markdown:
The implementation adds `validate_membership_type_code` for `allowed_membership_types`, preserving trim/lowercase normalization plus empty and 1024-character length checks while intentionally not enforcing the `gts.cf.core.rg.type.v1~` resource-group prefix. `TypeService::create_type` and `TypeService::update_type` now use that helper only for membership type entries; RG type code and parent type validation continue to use the existing prefix-specific validator.

Tests added/modified in `modules/system/resource-group/resource-group/tests/type_service_test.rs` cover the bug directly: create accepts a registered external domain membership type, update accepts a registered external domain membership type, create rejects an empty membership type entry, and update rejects an over-length membership type entry. Existing membership replacement tests continue to exercise RG-prefixed membership types for backwards compatibility.

The supplied CI log shows `cargo test -p cyberware-resource-group --test domain_unit_test` passing with 81 passed, 0 failed. I also ran the focused new regression tests locally with 4 passed, 0 failed, and then ran the full `type_service_test` suite with 49 passed, 0 failed. No blockers were found.
